reviews.review_text:
Resident evil village....well well well...

Walk into village.
Meet a vampire.
Meet a werewolf.
Meet a giant mutant.
Meet a giant baby.
Get chased through a factory by a guy who looks like he lost a fight against a hardware store.
Find a merchant.
Of course there's a merchant.
Buy ammo.
Buy food.
Buy a bigger gun.
Cook dinner.
Upgrade a shotgun.
Upgrade another shotgun.
Forget which shotgun you're using.
Open map.
Everything is red.
Close map.
Problem solved.
Feel safe.
Leave merchant.
Immediately regret it.
Find a key.
Need another key.
Need another key for the key.
Need another key for the key that unlocks the key.
Understand nothing.
Keep going.
At some point I just stopped asking questions.

U tought i got distracted by some big tiddyyyy goth/mommy vampires? Nah, there was toooo much bullsh*t. I love this game. ixdeee

Resident Evil Village is one of the first games that feels truly "next-gen," with absolutely stunning tech bolstered by the industry-leading RE Engine. Stunning visuals, truly bizarre creatures, a memorable parade of eccentric characters, and truly satisfying gameplay make Resident Evil Village soar, even if a few quirks in the plot delivery deflate the spectacle. Resident Evil Village is a truly excellent game and one of the best entries in the legendary franchise. Long may it continue.

What a masterpiece of a game, everything from the atmosphere to the gameplay and to the story. The story might be the best ever of any resident evil game so far, just perfect. I think village might be the most slept on resident evil game EVEN if it has sold millions of copies. Personally an A tier for me,

The only things i didnt enjoy abt the game was the final boss fight which felt less of horror and more like elden ring. i also really like the improvement in different enemy types from re7, apart from that i REALLY enjoyed the game. 

You should 100 percent consider purchasing the game!
Great game, Fantastic game. But y is the cover half chris half wolf. Makes no sense. 0/10. But big booby vampires 10/10
My first RE-game, and definitely not the last. Looks beautiful!

RE Village is a really decent game. I played it again after recently finishing the new RE9: Requiem, and gotta say I think in some aspects in comparison, this is a better game: weapon customization, level design, and the puzzles overall are better here than RE9. Having said that, I have a huge gripe with Ethan as the main protagonist. To put it shortly, and without spoiling anything, he is no classic RE protagonist. He doesn't hav the charisma of Chris, the coolness of Leon, the charm of Jill, or the attraction of Claire. For me, this alone brings the game a bit lower than it probably deserves in my personal RE ranking. But that's also a matter of taste. All in all, I definitely recommend it for a playthrough or two, and it hits almost all the other marks as a solid RE game.
Bro when i played it first time i got goosebumps bcz its graphic,action and story is amzing i would definetly recommend you to play It is a little bit more expensive but its worth it

wow wow WOW, this game exceeded my expectations. I caught myself just admiring the view/landscapes many times while playing it, this game is BEAUTIFUL. The graphics are amazing! This game and RE5 are my favorite of the RE franchise (haven't played RE9 yet) and honestly in all games in general, I can’t think of one thing I would change about this game. 10/10

UPDATE after playing shadows of rose: THIS HAS BECOME MY FAVORITE GAME, THE EMOTIONS IM CURRENTLY FEELING. I SOBBED IT'S SO GOOD. If you finish the game PLEASE play the DLC as well ITS WORTH TRUST.

Overall RE village is really fun to play, although i have my gripes with some things.

For example, Chris just not telling us anything about what is going on, and seeming like the bad guy until he reveals it, and even then its quite late in the story.
The last bossfight and ending just didnt do it for me, felt weak and bossfight was underwhelming.
FOV. Being locked to such a nauseous FOV was horrible, had to get an FOV mod after 1 hour of playing, otherwise i would have refunded it.

Everything else though, the graphics, the characters, the different areas, all very beautiful and fun  to explore. Except the dolls, ♥♥♥♥ the dolls.

Solid 7.5/10, would get it on sale if you can, if you try to explore everything the game is still only like 12 hours.

Easily the best Resident Evil game in my opinion. 
The ambience, atmosphere, and details keep you engaged in playing. 
The puzzles are slow paced which make you want to explore more and complete them.
The graphics are beautiful, maybe not the best like newest games, but they really fit for the game's atmosphere. 
I've learned everything about it, all the clues, puzzles, and secrets by heart
This game has my heart ♥

this game has great visuals and characters  with lot of value from exploring and solving size puzzled.
and has quite a lot of replay value is you like the bonus content such as concept art and getting infinite  ammo for your favourite weapons

I played and completed this game following the completion of RE 7. Expectations were high going in to the game, seeing as though RE 7 was a masterpiece. I was happily surprised with this game. I loved Ethan's story continuing and I feel as though he served a great purpose with great character development. The antagonists were great and made the story thrilling. The combat felt very smooth and enjoyable as well. Compared to RE 7, this game was more focused on the storyline and combat, rather than the horror aspect of the previous game. Overall, very happy with this game.
Weird but entertaining story, gameplay is fun

On a scale of RE5 to RE1 this game is solidly around RE4 in terms of action<->survival horror.

Really fun setpieces, fun enemies, mostly satisfying puzzles. Most of the characters are charming in some way, and all of the bosses are super fun. Guns are fun to shoot, the best movement in the franchise, and tons of attention to detail. Additionally, has a really strong horror section in house beneviento that is a masterclass in almost every way. Great optimization while still looking fantastic.

Village of Shadows is one of the most fun challenges the RE team has produced and I hope to see more like it.


Nitpick time:

Resource managment is nonexistent, sadly. I'm not sure if i've ever even run out of inventory space in any run before. I just wish your resources took up space in your attache case. Another thing i don't like it how linear a lot of sections are (Especially castle dimitrescu.) Don't give me the illusion of a bunch of different options and then only let me go 1 way at a time. 

Lady Dimitrescu is also a terrible, terrible tyrant. I've never even been hit by her once despite multiple playthroughs. You have to give her more movement options of her own if you're going to give the player such responsive movement and open enviroments.

DRM and the EULA are sketchy as hell, as per usual.

LET ME CHANGE THE FOV ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥

Lastly, the weapon "upgrades" are just extremely boring. When you get a new pistol or shotgun they just immidiately replace whatever you had before. The M1911 just outclasses the LEMI by every single metric. RE4 did this much better by making weapons sidegrades first and foremost (with the exception being the blacktail which just blows everything else out of the water, i suppose)


Overall, solid 8.5/10 game. I had a blast playing VoS | NG | No Bonuses. Well worth the money to play.

Really fun horror game with great action and creepy moments.
The graphics are amazing and every part of the game feels different.
One of my favorite Resident Evil games.
Great Ending to Ethan Winter's story. Not as scary as Biohazard but the storyline makes up for it.
Builds on the story of VII, but the combat feels a little better this time around. Also, crazy monster story tie in.

This was a huge missed opportunity to make a franchise defining trilogy. RE7 ended with Eveline, a child* raised as a bioweapon who desperately wanted a family. RE8 introduced Rose, another mold child who grew up with the loving family Eveline never had.  They are mirror simultaneously sharing a unique understanding of what its like to be part of the mold that they alone share.  A final game exploring that contrast where Eveline resents Rose for something neither of them had any control over is narrative gold. 

Instead, Capcom apparently decided that what RE7, the game that saved the franchise, was really missing was vampires, werewolves, giant zombie dragons, mech fights and airstrikes, as if the creative lead suffered an aneurysm while reviewing player survey data from five-year-olds.

👍 Recommended
🕒 14.3 hours on record
📅 Completed Campaign - 20th January 2026

Resident Evil Village was honestly an amazing experience from start to finish. The game balances horror and action really well, so it always keeps you interested without feeling repetitive.

The atmosphere is incredible and every location looks beautiful in its own creepy way. The graphics and sound design are top tier, especially when playing with headphones at night. Some sections genuinely had me stressed the whole time.

The story was surprisingly really good too, with a lot of twists that kept me hooked. The villains are super memorable, especially Lady Dimitrescu, and the boss fights were intense and fun. I also liked how the game improved enemy variety compared to RE7.

Ethan once again goes through absolute suffering because nobody knows how to communicate for more than 5 seconds.

The only thing I didn’t enjoy that much was the final boss fight since it felt more action-focused than horror, but overall the game was still fantastic.

Definitely recommend this game if you like horror games, Resident Evil, or just great single-player experiences in general.
Twas good game, good variety of levels and horror and action.

Spoilers: Resident Evil 8 felt very promising, the atmosphere is incredible and it takes place in my home country, I had very high hopes but sadly it falls short as a mainline game. Every lord is built up to be super important but they end up with so little screentime that you don't remember them but their environment. Lady Dimitrescu is by far the worst stalker enemy, with her covering like 2 rooms out of a whole castle without including scripted events where she appears and her death is dumb too, letting you freely stab her knowing you can regenerate/not die instantly. Bella, Cassandra and Daniela have basically 0 screentime with you killing Bela moments after you encounter her, they also work as stalker enemies but disappear quickly when you enter some rooms in the castle, they are so easily avoidable and have no personality other than "we are bad". Overall the castle is very short If you don't dilly dally and played games before in your life.

The Village was nice and there is a lot of love in the small details, being a local I saw many pots that I used in real life which was comical, it could have been bigger though, it takes you around 20 mins to loot everything. Donna Beneviento is such a nothing burger of a character, I know nothing about her other than she likes dolls and has a dead sister or something that is never mentioned. Her house is cool and she has an Ie, a traditional Romanian dress but that's about it. When people talk about her part of the game, they say "Oh yeah that baby is soooo scary!" Imagine being outshined by your underling.

Moreau's section is by far the most watered-down version (pun intended I'm funny like that). His part of the village was cut and now it serves as a gauntlet which you run through, meet him once, he pukes and then you fight him, his boss was also a nothing burger, move back and shoot and occasionally stand under rotten wood and wait. I don't know anything about Moreau other that he's a cry baby and made the Lycans. Idk what the Fihh of the group has in common with Varcolacs but apparently he does.

3 out of 4 lords you can cheese, seriously, their parts are so short. I get RE is a good game to speedrun but my god, if you try and play it normally that also feels like a speedrun, they are too short for a mainline game, It took longer to play than that travesty that RE Revelations 1 is.

Karl's part is by far the longest and frankly the most annoying, for a factory is was so claustrophobic, it did help but there was no horror in it, the Soldats were a nice addition but they are such missed opportunities, Karl is supposed to be insane, the Soldats could have been so body horror coded, instead you got bald Moroaice with visors, Soldats with drills instead of hands and just metal parts for armor on them, what?? It's so bad. Jet Soldats were cool but used only once in the entire game.

This might be the worst Resident Evil story, bar none. Chris comes in and tries to kill Miranda and kidnap Ethan... ?????? What? Chris Redfield, the same guy that has so much experience with Bio Weapons, was in so many situations like this one and made the worst possible choices ever again and again. He knew everything about the mold from the Baker estate, knew how unkillable the mold is and he found out about Miranda, who is so infected she can fully transform herself and fool people, yet he shot her couple of times and that was it. Did he even fight mold before???? I'm pretty sure there is a whole DLC in Re7 where CHRIS FOUGHT SO MANY MOLDED AND LUCAS BAKER AND HE STILL SHOT MIRANDA A COUPLE OF TIMES. He took brain damage I swear. We were also robbed of playing with all the Wolf Squad members in their descent into the Village, it would have been so cool but noooo we have to play with mister steroids over here who's mentally challenged. I knew nothing about Miranda up until the last section with Chris and her plan sucks too, putting the memories of your daughter in another corpse is not gonna make it your daughter again. Rose is different biologically and that will have an effect, I don't care that she's a grieving mother, when you're a scientist and you don't know simple stuff like that you're braindead. She's as smart as Moreau I swear. 

In conclusion, RE8 is a washed, stripped down to bones, liniar, short and unremarkable sequel to one of the best Resident Evil games out there. If you want a good Resident Evil Village, count down once and go play RE7. RE7 did so well and the devs chose to dial back the horror and the character personalities and try to replicate RE4... even though they can make a Remake of that. 

4/10 and those 3 points are bonus for Duke, he's the only good character imo.

RE 8 improves almost every aspect of RE 7 Biohazard, with a perfect balance between combat, puzzles and exploration. A Resident Evil that moves away from zombies in favor of gothic horror and European folklore was always going to be something unique and impressive, and Village certainly delivers.

Im too late for this but i highly recommend it the story is amazing and same for the gameplay but the ending is sad

10/10 no mistakes
If you have ever played a RE game, or like horror games, and you still have not jumped on Resident Evil Requiem, then I asked, what are you waiting for?

Now THIS is story, im not talking about some leon kennedy backflip bullshittery or chris "boulder" redfield, im talking about a normal person with a story about killing bioweapons, absolute 10/10 game can even run it on a gtx 1650 and an intel i5 and it STILL looks great.
Amazing game, everything about this game is worth buying and playing through at least once in your life
I found this comment on Yt, it goes something like this: "In every RE story the main character is a hero, all but Ethan Winters, he is just legendary."

excelent game ,grapghics are gorgeous ,art design, and optimisation the game itself gives an explanation for Resident evil 7 also you can feel when they made this they risked a lot of things ,they produce some ideas that feels off the series but still they made it logicall seeing wearwolfs , vampires like and so on it's something that we never seen from this franchise but overall the game is 9/10 , and the dlc is also interesting

I played this the first time on playstation and before i played i always believed it was one of the worst games in the franchise cause thats what everyone was saying, but its a total lie. it may not be the best, but its a really good game

RE8 (village) is honeslty one of the more less scary and more clsoe to being disgusting models but have some moments of scars and one is a big scare for some if you not ready for it. If you like re4 and the re9 (Requim) with just leon part then you will like this game. 
(sorry for the bad English :/ )
More action-based compared to its previous entry, but still fun regardless.

RE Village is one of the most enjoyable RE games I have played. The game constantly kept me on my toes and the story had me hooked from start to finish. Every area feels unique, from the eerie Castle Dimitrescu to the nightmare fuel that is House Beneviento. The atmosphere, sound design, and visuals are incredible, and the story kept me wanting to see what happened next. The game constantly introduced new enemies, environments, and mechanics, so it never feels repetitive. Combat is satisfying and exploration is rewarding. 10/10 would recommend to anyone that is looking for something that is action-packed and SO fun to play

i love the this game its long and have every thing you want the . world the .fib . story. plotiwist . you will cry you . 
if you want to play full exprince i will rec play7 first and the fights <3

i recommend it 100/10

This game is a tad funky but overall it still has lots of resident evil magic. My main complaint would be that there was hardly any inventory management—I always enjoy that aspect of RE games. Lots of cool sights to see though, that ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up baby section in the basement of the Beneviento estate had me actually terrified

Bosses: Big vampire goth girl, doll freak, Ugly fish, Old cowboy man like magneto, Cult roleplay girl obsessed with a baby

GOOD STORY with maniac antagonist!
PEAK ETHAN!

Great Game
Noticable improment from 7 just missing the item which allows us to see the loot scattered across the map
This game's kinda weird, idk how to put it, fun but sucks on replays for 100%

A somewhat soft thumbs up. 

I have issues with the plot, characters and storytelling, particularly in how RE7 was continued and also some impacts to greater RE-verse.

I also think they could have done a much better job with the combat, considering they had an excellent foundation already in RE7's Not A Hero DLC but then stepped back from it inexplicably. 

Still, it's a solid game with some good mechanics and variety. Quite nice looking, making it immersive and scary, and runs well, to boot.
Fantastic game, the developers understood what they were doing. This game is really beautiful, and scary lol. Can't wait to play the next game too.

The Winter's storyline is a welcome addition to the franchise.   House Beneviento was definitely my favorite part in this entry.  

Compared to other modern Resident Evil games (RE2R, RE3R, RE4R, and RE7) I found Resident Evil Village to be more unstable on Linux.  Specifically, when going through Heisenberg's Factory - The game would often freeze and crash my desktop environment.  Outside of this, the base game and DLC worked without issue.

Better than 7, ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ I didn't know I would enjoy it so much.
Goth mommies, guns, saving a child, playing as ruthless and badass father, CHRIS REDFIELD TOO.
Loved boss fights, the whole story is just nice and fitting.
Would love the alternative ending where Ethan joined Heisenberg, it would be interesting outcome.
11/10

The villains are incredibly cool, with fascinating lore that ties deeply into the Resident Evil universe as a whole. The overall art design and visuals look amazing. I especially loved how a certain part of the game completely shifts from action into pure psychological horror, and the DLC is a fantastic addition too. And as usual with Capcom games it is very well optimized.

This one feels like such a great step up from 7, with Ethan having shown clear advancement from the previous game.

The environment and aesthetic of Village really calls back to the nostalgia of RE4 with even a few references to that game being made.

The bosses all have real flavor to them, the enemy types are diverse, the story is really gripping

Really, if I had anything negative to say about it, i'd say my only complaint is that the writing centering around Chris is a little annoying.
For some reason, they have him holding the idiot ball for most of the game, setting up a major betrayal from him and he just REFUSES to take ten seconds to explain himself. 

I know they wanted to really set up a mystery with the Chris subplot but all it ended up doing is making Chris look like a moron with all his "no time to explain" stuff

But that aside? One of my fave RE games, hands down.

Genuinely a fantastic game. Takes what worked from 7 and improved on it 10 fold.  Ethan is more developed naturally from where he left off, and in terms of gameplay you can really kick tons of ass while still being pretty spooky at times. Only criticism is that, while it is longer than 7 its still fairly short and I'd say it is frontloaded in terms of quality levels(as in, the first level after the intro is probably the best in the game). But the other "levels" are still really good too.

All in all, a classic and easily one of the best games of the 2020's still.

Liked it a lot, very evocative of RE4. Wish puzzles were a little more complex. Story didn't quite make sense, but that's RE. Combat was awesome. Game is beautiful. Voice acting for all characters except Heisenburg was really good. I think Miranda would have been a more effective villain if she was more present the entire game.

mold mold mold, RE8 has the best protagonist because he loves mold as much as me 

There's a lot of mold in this game and the antagonists are just as moldy. Very moldy atmosphere with  engaging combat against moldy werewolves and women who somehow got special powers from the mold, which naturally makes sense. With detailed set pieces and good pacing throughout if you liked the mold from Louisiana, you're going to enjoy the one in Romania.
Great graphics and a great story. A lot to do in this game.
its very fun and entertaining i prefer getting the third person view it makes the game way more fun

Good game if you're an RE fan

First half of the game is very good
But after castle everything go meh
Resident evil village is truly a solid game easily a 9.5/10. its my favorite in the series i honestly have no complaints at all

Absolute 10/10 masterpiece might be my favourite resident evil

The opening village of shadows movie is really cool and coraline esque

The atmosphere and graphics are absolutely top tier

Lycan's are gnarly as hell

Heisenberg is a great villain

This dimitrescu family got some good genes

Where my storage box at

A lot of yellow paint around this village

Shadows of Rose DLC is also fantastic and a brings a very nice and comforting ending to the Ethan games
Very addictive game with great atmosphere design, intriguing story with a lot of plot twists and unique level designs. Could be a bit longer perhaps but I'm very satisfied overall.

One of the best compliments I can give to this game is that it doesn't feel dragged out. You're not being slowly dragged through a long list of tasks just to keep you around for a bit longer. The story is concise, the levels are varied, and the gameplay is fun. I did ship my pants a few times, but the game does a good job of giving your a breather after an intense moment — it's actually really good at creating that balance.

I really enjoyed how often the story took a turn. It kept things interesting and prevented a feeling of "going through a slog" to get to the next place. I was often chasing the next thing before I expected to be.

The controls felt incredibly floaty and delayed (controller) until I disabled acceleration, which fixed it entirely. I had to get used to the low FOV, but I suppose it's a design decision to make things appear more "in your face". 

Great game!
Great game. Loved the atmosphere, the ambience and the lore. Somehow it brings back the feelings I had while I was a kid playing PS2 for the first time, not sure how to describe it.

Never thought I would actually feel sad after finishing a Resident Evil game but here we are. Significant improvement over RE 7. 

10/10

i havent even finished the game yet and this is my favorite re game the only gripe i have with the game is the gun shots are really puny feeling but other than that i have nothing. lowkey the best re game ive played better than all the other re games just saying its better than all the other games its the best re game ive playe out of re2 3 4 8 9 all remakes i dont play the older ones because i like the remakes better because i havent played the older games yet bit i probably will eventually because i like the franchise of resident evil and im listening to music while i type this and my fingers turned purple and blue so yeah thats about it see ya
I screamed, I pause, I searched tutorial on YouTube, I came back what a peak gameplay 10/10.....

this game was truly goated, this game was a blast to play, the story was great, i loved all of the characters, although the initial encounter with chris was like what the hell? the ending to this game was perfect. it was sad as hell. the game overall is a 10/10, gameplay wise was great and story wise was great. i definately recommend this game. however i also recommend that you play 7 before this one, certain things will make more sense if you do.
very good game lots of cool enemies great story play RE 7 before this for story purposes.

Capcom is cooking and they continue to deliver engaging and thrilling stories filled with addictive gameplay loop elements and characters. Usually a game series dies after the third installment but they fact they kept going this long and the games only got better is a testament to the leadership and vision of Resident Evil. Now on to Requiem!!!

I have played almost every major Resident Evil game:
RE0, RE1, RE2 Remake, RE3 Remake, RE4 OG + Remake, RE7, and now RE8.

And I can confidently say this:

Resident Evil Village is one of the greatest games I have ever played. Absolute cinema.

This game feels like the perfect fusion of Resident Evil 4 and Resident Evil 7. It has the progression, exploration, merchant system, and action pacing of RE4, while also carrying the horror, atmosphere, emotional storytelling, and psychological tension of RE7.

The atmosphere is INSANE. The way the village changes as the story progresses, the snow reducing, the environments evolving, the feeling that the world itself is reacting to your progression — it all adds so much immersion.

Every area feels unique:
the castle, Beneviento house, Moreau section, Heisenberg’s factory — all completely different flavors of horror and storytelling.

And the way Capcom connected everything back to the older Resident Evil lore, Umbrella, Spencer, and the franchise history was honestly genius. At first the game feels disconnected from classic Resident Evil, but slowly everything clicks together in such a satisfying way.

Ethan Winters is also one of the most underrated protagonists in gaming. He feels human. Not some invincible superhero, just a guy desperately trying to survive and protect his family. That emotional grounding is what makes the story hit so hard.

I played this game alongside my friend by syncing our progression together on our laptops, and the experience became unforgettable. We literally played till 1 AM discussing every reveal, every boss fight, every twist, and every insane cinematic moment.

The Heisenberg factory section especially felt overwhelming in the best possible way. We had to stop and continue the next day because of how intense the experience became.

This game is not just “a horror game.”
It is survival horror, action, psychological horror, emotional drama, gothic fantasy, and blockbuster cinema combined together.

RE4 may still be the revolutionary gameplay masterpiece, but for me, RE7 and especially RE8 are on another level narratively and atmospherically.

Even if this game was not called Resident Evil and was released as a completely new franchise, I would still praise it the exact same way.

Absolute masterpiece.
Absolute cinema.
GOATED game.
7/10 พยายามจะเป็น RE4 ผสมดาร์คโซลส์หน่อย ๆ แต่คอนเท้นต์มันไม่ค่อยมีอะไรให้น่าเล่นซ้ำเท่าไหร่ เนื้อเรื่องไม่ได้ดีเด่นอะไร ออกจะกาว ๆ ด้วย ช่วงแรกใส่ชาวบ้านมาทำไมก็ไม่รู้
Great action packed game and support for 32:9 and OLEDs.

Really fun game. I think I enjoyed RE7 a bit more than this, but it was a nice addition to Ethan's story. The best part of the game was definitely the doll house for me. It had me at the edge of my seat at certain parts, and overall it was a quick but really fun segment. I felt it was the only part that actually had me kind of scared. Also enjoyed Castle Dimetrescu, the boss fights were great, and the whole area looked really cool. The game overall had a lot of fun puzzles, and good gameplay.

Really liked this one. The graphic is beautiful, the story is super engaging, and the villains are incredibly unique in terms of both design and personality. Ethan Winters show much more personality in this game compared to RE7 and easily became one of my favorite RE protagonist. 8.5/10

A great closure to the story of Resident Evil 7, blending together elements from both that game and RE4. The setting is one of my favourites, although in some parts it feels very similar to 4. I also like how they brought back the shop system; the sense of strategy and progression is really enjoyable. The gameplay, ambience and level design is amazing as usual.

There are some issues I found, though. The difficulty is definitely toned down, and it never really feels like you're in danger. This is especially noticeable in the DLC, where you're literally playing as a mostly defenseless girl.
There's also the yellow paint epidemic, where every tiny interactable key item or path you need to use is very obviously marked to direct the player toward it. They pretty much baby-proofed the game, which hurts the sense of accomplishment.
The story in the main game is good. Shadows of Rose felt more predictable and a bit corny, but it was fine overall.

But the biggest issue of all: not enough Lady D moments. 0/10.

But yeah, good game, would play the castle section with one hand again.

Resident Evil Village feels like a dark fairytale disguised as a survival horror game.

This game somehow balances horror, action, mystery, emotional storytelling, and absolute insanity all at the same time — and it works surprisingly well.

From the moment the game starts, the atmosphere pulls you in. The snowy village, the abandoned houses, the distant sounds, the gothic architecture — everything feels eerie and immersive. Capcom absolutely nailed the visual design here. Even years later, this game still looks incredible.

And then there’s Castle Dimitrescu.

Walking through those giant halls while trying not to get hunted down by a 9-foot vampire woman is somehow both terrifying and stressful 😭

But what makes Village special is how varied the experience feels. Every major area has its own personality:

Castle Dimitrescu = gothic horror
House Beneviento = psychological nightmare fuel 💀
Moreau’s section = creepy sadness
Heisenberg’s factory = industrial chaos

The game constantly changes its tone without losing its identity, and that keeps the experience fresh from beginning to end.

Gameplay feels fantastic too. Combat is satisfying, weapons feel impactful, exploration is rewarding, and upgrading your gear through the Duke creates that addictive “just one more upgrade” feeling.

Speaking of the Duke:

“What’re ya buyin’?”
Wrong game.
Still legendary energy 😂

Ethan Winters also became a much stronger protagonist here compared to Resident Evil 7. His determination to protect his daughter genuinely gives the story emotional weight beneath all the horror and madness.

And honestly, House Beneviento deserves special mention because that section was one of the most terrifying experiences I’ve had in gaming in YEARS.

If you know… you know. 👶💀

The sound design is incredible too. Quiet footsteps, distant noises, creaking wood, sudden screams — this game understands how to build tension without constantly relying on cheap jumpscares.

Of course, it’s not perfect:

Some action-heavy sections feel less “survival horror”
Certain boss fights can be frustrating
The story becomes wonderfully ridiculous near the end 😂
And yes, inventory management will make you feel like you’re solving advanced mathematics with shotgun shells

But overall?

Resident Evil Village is one of the best modern Resident Evil games and one of the most memorable horror experiences in recent years.

It’s creepy, beautiful, emotional, intense, weird, and completely unforgettable.

Verdict:
A fantastic blend of survival horror and action with amazing atmosphere, memorable villains, emotional storytelling, and enough nightmare fuel to make you check dark corners in real life 🌑

10/10 would panic while opening doors again 🚪💀

This is the horror game I dreamed about playing as a kid. It has everything, and somehow all of the different elements come together and the world feels genuine and unique. Great visual style, and such a perfect atmosphere/setting. The town feels like its own living, breathing character. A few memorable characters. Fun blend of exploration, action, and horror build-up. Typical RE style puzzles. This is probably my favorite horror game of all time.
There's some really scary segments to story and I still say re7 quite more scary. Overall great game!!

A great close to Ethan Winters' saga. From start to finish this game has you on edge, never knowing what's going to be thrown at you next... Also infamous for the scariest moment in Resident Evil/Gaming history... if you know you know. This game had such a strong impact on me, I named my son Ethan. I hope my boy one day plays this game when Dad's no longer around and understands how much I love him. Anyway, amazing game, absolutely worth getting, mercenaries is addicting as heck.
Just an amazing and beautiful game. Massive game play improvement from RE7. Doesn't feel like Ethan games though.

prety fun but chris redfield fit is so ahh bro he does not fit innat turtle neck bro that cannot be comfortable also story is very very good and heisenburg boss was sick i felt like that image of beast boy going crazy on the go kart

An incredible follow-up to Resident Evil 7. Great level design, solid enemy variety, a really good story (especially as far as RE games go), and the best horror segment that I've experienced up to this point in the series. Not quite as puzzly(?) as I would've liked but as far as complaints go that's about all I can muster. 

Another huge plus was the boss fights, there are a grand total of four in the base game and I'd say all but one was great, about on the same level as the RE4 bosses when considering only gameplay, but going beyond I think 8's take the cake. The spectacle and bosses' personalities are just about the best in the series so far. Not that any one individually beats out Wesker but that's a high bar. 

Bringing a merchant back is always great as it makes more playstyles viable and adds a tangible sense of progression beyond just acquiring new weapons. There are plenty of little "side quests" and treasure hunts to go on beyond the main objectives which is always welcome, although it makes me wonder if you'd feel strapped for resources if you didn't go out of your way for most of them, but I had an abundance of crafting mats by the end of both playthroughs so it's hard to imagine that being the case.

This game was a great follow up to Resident Evil 7. I don't think it's as scary or memorable as 7 was but I would still recommend it since it was a lot of fun. Especially if you liked Resident Evil 7.

Play this after 7. And please wait for a sale before you buy it
my fave game OAT!! have now 100% on multiple devices and it never gets old. ethan winters the man you are

I played this game 5 years after it's release, I was not playing much games when this was released, and later within 1-2 years I got spoiled with the plot and THAT reveal, which made me lost my interest to play this game, but I did wanted it, so I played it, and this game is a really good successor of it's last part, and just like RE7, 8 is also a masterpiece if you are someone who are not spoiled with the plot like me, regardless I will still call this game a masterpiece, also RE7 is the most scariest RE for me, maybe because of First Person but I played 8 in Third Person as I'm a scaredy cat tbh, still it was scary in some parts especially that House Beneviento part, in both the main story and even more in the DLC, Ethan is the GOAT man, the emotional aspect of this part was also very good, really loved the story, the new enemies, and also, the Village parts made me really feel like RE4, even Duke was like the merchant from it, I guess this should be it, in short, just play this game, it's really a masterpiece.

This game is some kind of fusion between the gameplay of RE4 and RE7. Tho I personally prefer the gameplay of 4 if you asked me.

It's a good continuation of the story of RE7's character. In some way this game is basically RE7 part 2

Personally I think it's an ok game

6/10

The game isn't scary. It has a lot of similarities to Re4. Over the shoulder is locked behind dlc. The vampires seem random for a RE game but it is obvious that there just trying to imitate Re4 but fails to capture this tone in first person. This just like Re7 is just another attempt at avoiding going back to classic RE roots. These games went from experiments going wrong to some Diablo story style knockoff. If this didn't got RE in the name I wouldn't even know it was part of the same franchise.

Playing this without finishing RE7 isn't that bad but in most cases people think it's necessary. The game provides enough context to understand what's going on. This was phenomenal to play. I loved it quite bit. There isn't much to criticize for it, I enjoyed it. Even though I skipped finishing the previous game, I might consider going back to it. All in all, this was a 8.9/10

Going from RE4 Remake (action shooter flavor gameplay) to RE8 Village (slower horror), I found the game a bit underwhelming; however, after a few sessions and towards the end the game's pacing felt better. 

I never played RE7, but there is an optional RE7 story recap video when playing for the first time to build context for RE8.

Con:
Like RE4, there are multiple "maps" that you will lose access to as you progress the game. My gripe is that the game does not tell you when you will lose access and you end up missing out on treasures &  exploration. It isn't until the final boss fight of the game that "the Merchant" guy gives you the hint of being it your last time (and even then the surrounding map is closed off except for the direction to the boss fight). /// If you are the type of person who tends to play once and uninstall, I would recommend looking up the boss fight "triggers" to avoid (and yes, that will be putting you in the spoiler zone unfortunately).

Can't view concept art/figures/bonus modes until after completing the game once.

I will play the DLC when it goes on sale (currently $20).
I would wait to purchase the game on a sale (currently $40)

The first time I saw this game, I thought it would not be my cup of tea. "Lycans" and "Vampires", really? 
But I changed my mind. It's a nice game! Feels balanced and unforgiving at the same time and gives a nice conclusion to the story of one the characters of the series. 

Good graphics, atmosphere, controls, etc etc, the pattern for most RE games. Happy this one is also in this group.

Great game and a great sequel to 7. In my opinion, the best intro to the Resident Evil franchise would be to play 7 and then 8. The two of them together kind of just show everything that you play the RE franchise for. You get the horror, the action, the goofiness, all in one very approachable and accessible package. I think 8 particularly does a good job at getting everything. 
Personally, I have 7 in my top 3 games in the series, so I was really looking forward to this one. I think 7 is a better game overall, but I think 8's greatest strength is that it kind of just does everything well. The story is great, the iconic Resident Evil goofiness is there, the combat is satisfying, the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ baby section is one of the scariest experiences of any game I've ever played, and the action sequences are awesome as hell. It's a game that just has a little bit of everything.

Game ran great on my rig and as a long time RE fan I couldn't pass this up when it went on sale. This game exceeded my expectations when it came to being a proper horror game and is a good follow up to 7.

I wasn’t a huge fan of Resident Evil 7, but I still wanted to give Resident Evil Village a try.

I really liked the similarities to RE4, and continuing Ethan’s story felt great. I started the game pretty skeptical, but by the end I was surprisingly pleased with how the story turned out.

The Shadows of Rose DLC was also very different from a usual Resident Evil experience, but it felt fresh and was genuinely fun to play.

Overall, a really solid game and, in my opinion, a much better experience than RE7.

After completing Requiem on Ps5, I wanted to play other resident evil games. This is my 3rd ResiEvil game (second was Re7), such a scary game yet I love every second of it! Would 100% recommend playing this game!
It was fun. Probably the least "horror game" of the series, but I am not a horror fan so great game.

RE8 IS SOOOO GOOOOODD!!!

Ethan Winters is DEFINITELY Top 3 Resident Evil characters its not even close. Story is thorough and brutal. Gameplay is fun, immersive, and intriguing. Characters are fleshed out with detail and emotion. Everything ab this game is awesome.
Fun gameplay, incredible story and graphics. Ive replayed so many times, easily my favorite re game.

Its was everything, and more than I expected, i played on hardcore mode as i play most of the RE games on hardcore to get the experience the developers intented us to get, but I will say that in in village the intro and mother mirand fight was hard and time consuming, especially the mother miranda fight fk it took me 3 hours to beat her, got my sekiro memories back ;) , but otherwise it's an amazing installment in re series, capcom never disappoints.

Very good Re game if you compare to Re 6. Not that scary if you compare to Re Biohazard but it has its peaks. Expansions pack is also very good and worth playing as it adds to the story of Ethan Winters.
Super fun Resi game. so much good to say about just a blast honestly simple as that.

Great game. Finished it in one sitting. 100% recommend. I would wait until it goes on sale though because the current price is a little steep, and it goes on sale fairly often.

Enjoyed the first and middle part of the game. The ending however not so much.

Still a great experience.

This game is very peak, my personal favorite out of every RE game, the story is amazing, Ethan gets much need depth and character development, the boss fights were good, the lycans (werewolf guys) were very intimidating at first, also the total atmosphere and setting of winter Romania is a very cool and interesting setting I love the snow everywhere, the Duke is goated, the Chris section was pretty cool, the villain cast was very good and intimidating (not counting Moreau), I believe this game has infinite replayability, the ending is very heartbreaking and sad (in a good way), there ain't no winter colder than Ethan Winters absolutely do recommend this game. 9.3/10

I really enjoyed RE Village. I feel like it takes RE7, mixes in RE4, and you end up with something that's truly fun to play through, although not perfect. 

The enemy variety has massively improved from RE7, and keeps incredible environmental design. The villains you face and their own unique areas keep things interesting the whole way through. Dimitrescu's and Heisenberg's sections are all incredible and something that will stick with you from this game, and are memorable and fantastic. And the house section is one of the few times this franchise is genuinely terrifying, albeit not so scary on a second playthrough. 

Although the plot is interesting, the story and dialogue, as usual, are nothing to ride home about.

I did have a few nitpicks with it though. Despite having a technically limited inventory, at no point did I ever struggle with inventory management, which I feel is something that needs to be looked at for future titles. Additionally, there is a drop in quality after the house section, dealing with Moreau and the Lycans. Once you've done Moreau's section once, it is generally pretty boring afterwards, and the Lycan section feels generally uninspired and a bit bland. Additionally, I'm not sure what happened to the audio of the weapons, but none of them sound very good. 

Separate section for the Shadows of Rose DLC, it's terrible. The premise is interesting, but execution not very good. I was just ready for it to be over by the time it was done.

The 100% is also not very hard at all.

Overall, really enjoyed my time with Village, and hope to see more like it in the future.
